phrasal_verbs.meaning: if something centres around someone or something, or you centre something around them, they are its main subject of attention or interest
start enjoying something or become enthusiastic about it; if a train, plane, etc gets into a place, it arrives there; become involved in a bad situation
enjoy a particular thing or activity; choose something as a subject of study or as your career
stop liking someone or something; explode or be fired; leave a place, especially for a particular purpose; if food or drink goes off, it is no longer fresh
if something or someone grows on you, you start to like them more
spend time in a particular place or with particular people; lean out of a window so that the top part of your body is outside
continue to do something; move at the same speed as someone or something
relax and enjoy yourself, doing no work
spend time doing things in a relaxed way; behave in a silly way
learn a new skill or start a habit without intending to; go and meet someone or something that you have arranged to take somewhere in a vehicle
spend a particular amount of time doing something, or make a particular amount of effort in order to do something
find someone or something by looking for them in a determined way
have a particular amount of time away from work; become successful or popular very fast
take someone to a place like a cinema or a restaurant and usually pay for them
prepare for a sport or activity by doing gentle exercises or practising just before it starts

like: it’s not like sb to do
off: have / take / be given the day off
have / take / be given time off

idioms.meaning: with nothing in particular to do
someone who spends a lot of time sitting at home watching television
do because of a sudden feeling that you must have or do something
have more time available than you need
relax and enjoy yourself because you are in a comfortable environment

idioms.idiom: life and soul of the party
idioms.meaning: someone who is very lively at social events
sit down and relax, especially with your feet raised off the ground
rest after being very busy, so that you will be ready to start working again
decide that you want something very much

idioms.idiom: variety is the spice of life
idioms.meaning: used for saying that different experiences and situations make life enjoyable or interesting

J.title: Original course practice
J.passage:
Original passage for Preference and Leisure Activities

Marco joined a morning tutorial because the group wanted fresh practice for course practice. The tutor wrote (1) ...... (2) ...... (3) ...... (4) ...... (5) ...... on the board and asked everyone to explain their choices. Later, the group compared (6) ...... (7) ...... (8) ...... (9) ...... (10) ...... with the rule from the lesson. The text is new, but it practises the same course practice.
